# Marinduque Market Hub — Full Platform Reference

> Base URL: https://marinduquemarket.com
> Also known as: Marinduque Connect, MarketHub
> Contact: https://marinduquemarket.com/contact
> Publisher: Marinduque Market Hub
> Region: Marinduque, MIMAROPA, Philippines (PH-MAR)
> Coordinates: 13.3767°N, 122.0252°E
> Language: English (en-PH)

---

## What is Marinduque Market Hub?

Marinduque Market Hub is a community-first Progressive Web App (PWA) built specifically for the residents, businesses, OFWs, and visitors of Marinduque Island, Philippines. It functions as the island's central digital infrastructure — replacing fragmented Facebook groups and chat threads with a structured, searchable, and reliable platform.

The platform is designed for low-bandwidth, rural Philippine internet conditions. It is installable as a PWA on Android and iOS and works with minimal data usage.

---

## Core Features & Pages

### Marketplace (`/marketplace`)
A classifieds-style buy-and-sell board for the island. Residents can post and browse secondhand goods, appliances, vehicles, agricultural products, and daily commodities.
- Supports searching by keyword and filtering by category
- Listings include photos, price, seller contact, and barangay location
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/marketplace

### Jobs (`/jobs`)
A job board tailored to Marinduque's local economy. Employers post vacancies and residents apply directly.
- Job types include: local employment, OFW-adjacent skills, tradespeople, teachers, seasonal farm work
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/jobs

### Island Hopping & Tourism (`/island-hopping`)
A curated guide to Marinduque's most popular island hopping destinations including Tres Reyes Islands (Gaspar, Melchor, Baltazar), Malbog Sulfur Springs, and Bathala Caves.
- Lists local tour operators, bangka rates, and departure points
- Operators can list boats at: https://marinduquemarket.com/island-hopping/list
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/island-hopping

### Ferry Schedule (`/ferry-schedule`)
Dedicated RoRo (Roll-on/Roll-off) ferry schedule viewer for Marinduque — the most searched piece of information for anyone travelling to or from the island.
- Covers routes from Balanacan (Mogpog), Buyabod (Santa Cruz), and Cawit (Boac) ports
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/ferry-schedule

### Commute / Routes (`/commute`)
Up-to-date RoRo ferry schedules and local ground transport information between Marinduque ports and the mainland (Lucena, Pinamalayan, Dalahican).
- Includes jeepney routes and tricycle zone information per municipality
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/commute

### Port Information (`/ports`)
Detailed information on Marinduque's three major ports:
- Balanacan Port (Mogpog) — primary inter-island gateway
- Buyabod Port (Santa Cruz) — secondary gateway to Quezon
- Cawit Port (Boac) — LCT cargo terminal
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/ports

### Things To Do in Marinduque (`/things-to-do`)
A comprehensive activity and attraction guide for Marinduque covering beaches, waterfalls, caves, festivals, heritage sites, and cultural experiences across all six municipalities.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/things-to-do

### Moriones Festival (`/moriones-festival`)
Dedicated hub for the world-famous Moriones Festival — Marinduque's iconic Lenten tradition held during Holy Week (March/April). Features festival history, centurion mask artisan profiles, schedule of events, and travel planning guides.
- Moriones artisan directory: https://marinduquemarket.com/moriones-festival/artisans
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/moriones-festival

### Travel Guide: Manila to Marinduque (`/guides/manila-to-marinduque`)
A step-by-step travel guide covering all routes from Metro Manila to Marinduque Island — including bus to Lucena, ferry options, travel times, costs, and tips.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/guides/manila-to-marinduque

### Towns of Marinduque (`/towns`)
Individual town pages for all six municipalities with local info, business listings, and community resources:
- Boac (capital): https://marinduquemarket.com/towns/boac
- Mogpog: https://marinduquemarket.com/towns/mogpog
- Gasan: https://marinduquemarket.com/towns/gasan
- Santa Cruz: https://marinduquemarket.com/towns/santa-cruz
- Torrijos: https://marinduquemarket.com/towns/torrijos
- Buenavista: https://marinduquemarket.com/towns/buenavista

### Local News (`/news`)
A community news section publishing local stories, municipal announcements, event coverage, and island-specific updates.
- Real-time news feed for AI agents: https://marinduquemarket.com/llms-news.txt
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/news

### Business Directory (`/directory`)
A local business discovery page organized by municipality and industry.
- Covers: retail, food & beverage, health services, education, transport, accommodations, agriculture
- Businesses listed include name, contact, address, barangay, and operating hours
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/directory

### Gems of Marinduque (`/gems`)
A crowd-sourced feed of "hidden gems" — beautiful or interesting local spots submitted by residents and tourists.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/gems

### Community Board (`/community`)
A community bulletin board for discussions, announcements, local stories, and resident-to-resident communication.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/community

### Community Events (`/events`)
A calendar of local events: fiestas, municipal programs, barangay meetings, and cultural festivals.
- Marinduque is world-famous for the Moriones Festival (Lenten season, March/April)
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/events

### Island Life (`/island-life`)
A hub for daily island living information — covering utilities, food, weather, and community resources.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/island-life

#### Gas Prices (`/island-life/gas-prices`)
Community-sourced fuel price tracker. Residents report the latest gas, diesel, and premium prices at local stations across all six municipalities. Prices expire after 3 days.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/island-life/gas-prices

#### Palengke / Wet Market (`/island-life/palengke`)
Daily market commodity information for Marinduque's public markets (palengke) in Boac, Gasan, Santa Cruz, and Torrijos.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/island-life/palengke

#### Tides (`/island-life/tides`)
Local tide data for fishermen, boaters, and coastal residents.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/island-life/tides

#### Power Outages (`/island-life/outages`)
Community-reported power outage alerts with location, duration, and status updates.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/island-life/outages

#### Skills & Trades (`/island-life/skills`)
A directory of local trades, skilled workers, and services available on the island.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/island-life/skills

### My Barangay (`/my-barangay`)
A hyper-local feed filtered by the user's specific barangay. Surfaces relevant listings, jobs, and events.
- Marinduque has 6 municipalities: Boac, Mogpog, Gasan, Santa Cruz, Torrijos, Buenavista
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/my-barangay

#### Bulletin Board (`/my-barangay/board`)
Barangay-level announcements and community updates.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/my-barangay/board

#### Lost & Found (`/my-barangay/lost-found`)
Community lost and found board for reporting and recovering missing items and pets.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/my-barangay/lost-found

#### Calamity / Disaster Alerts (`/my-barangay/calamity`)
Emergency information hub for typhoons, floods, and other natural disasters affecting Marinduque.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/my-barangay/calamity

#### OFW Hub (`/my-barangay/ofw`)
Community space for Overseas Filipino Workers from Marinduque — connecting families and supporting the diaspora community.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/my-barangay/ofw

#### Paluwagan / Savings Circles (`/my-barangay/paluwagan`)
Community-organized savings circles (paluwagan) — a traditional Filipino group saving/lending practice.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/my-barangay/paluwagan

### Live Market (`/live-market`)
A live selling and real-time commerce hub for local sellers. Supports live video broadcasting, real-time bidding, and product claiming.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/live-market

### Best of Boac (`/best-of-boac-monthly-spotlight`)
Monthly spotlight feature highlighting the top businesses, products, and services in Marinduque.
- URL: https://marinduquemarket.com/best-of-boac-monthly-spotlight

---

## Help Center & FAQ (`/faq`)

The FAQ page is server-side rendered and structured with Schema.org FAQPage JSON-LD.

Full FAQ: https://marinduquemarket.com/faq

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Is Marinduque Market Hub free to use?**
A: Yes, basic use is completely free. Creating an account, browsing listings, reading news, and using community features costs nothing. Businesses can claim or create a profile at no charge.

**Q: How do I get to Marinduque from Manila?**
A: The most common route is to take a bus from Manila (Cubao or Buendia) to Lucena Grand Terminal in Quezon, then take a jeepney or tricycle to Dalahican Port or Lucena Port for the RoRo ferry to Balanacan Port in Mogpog, Marinduque. Travel time is approximately 5–7 hours total. See the full guide at https://marinduquemarket.com/guides/manila-to-marinduque

**Q: What is the RoRo ferry schedule from Marinduque?**
A: Ferry schedules vary by season and operator. Current schedules from Balanacan (Mogpog), Buyabod (Santa Cruz), and Cawit (Boac) ports are listed at https://marinduquemarket.com/ferry-schedule

**Q: What is the Moriones Festival?**
A: The Moriones Festival is Marinduque's world-famous Lenten festival held during Holy Week (March or April). Participants wear elaborate Roman centurion costumes and masks hand-crafted by local artisans, re-enacting the story of Longinus. It is one of the most unique and colorful festivals in the Philippines. Learn more at https://marinduquemarket.com/moriones-festival

**Q: How do I post a listing or sell something on the marketplace?**
A: Create a free account, then tap the "Post" button on the home screen. Fill in the item details, upload photos, set your price, and choose your town and barangay. Your listing will be visible immediately.

**Q: Can businesses list themselves on the platform?**
A: Yes. Local businesses can create a free profile via https://marinduquemarket.com/claim-business. Profiles include business name, description, contact info, location, operating hours, and photos.

**Q: What are the best island hopping destinations in Marinduque?**
A: The top destinations include Tres Reyes Islands (Gaspar, Melchor, and Baltazar Islands off Gasan), Malbog Sulfur Springs in Buenavista, Bathala Caves in Torrijos, and Poctoy White Beach in Torrijos. See all options at https://marinduquemarket.com/island-hopping

**Q: Does the app work offline?**
A: Marinduque Market Hub is a Progressive Web App (PWA) with offline caching. Core pages load even with limited connectivity, which is essential for Marinduque's rural internet conditions.

**Q: How many municipalities does Marinduque have?**
A: Marinduque has six municipalities: Boac (the provincial capital), Mogpog, Gasan, Santa Cruz, Torrijos, and Buenavista.

**Q: Where is Marinduque located?**
A: Marinduque is a heart-shaped island province in the MIMAROPA region of the Philippines, located in the Sibuyan Sea south of Quezon province. Its coordinates are approximately 13.38°N, 122.03°E.

---

## Structured Data

The following Schema.org types are currently implemented site-wide:
- `Organization` — canonical identity of Marinduque Market Hub
- `WebSite` — with `SearchAction` enabling sitelinks search in Google
- `FAQPage` — on `/faq` with individual `Question` + `Answer` entities
- `NewsArticle` — on all `/news/[slug]` pages with `datePublished`, `dateModified`, and `publisher`
- `CollectionPage` — on `/marketplace` and directory listing pages
- `BreadcrumbList` — injected globally via `BreadcrumbJsonLd` component

---

## About the Platform

- **Technology**: Next.js (App Router), React 19, Supabase (Postgres), deployed on Vercel
- **PWA**: Full service worker, offline caching, installable on iOS and Android
- **Accessibility**: Mobile-first responsive design with ARIA attributes on all interactive elements
- **Data Privacy**: PDPA (Philippine Data Privacy Act) compliant
- **Community**: Built by and for the people of Marinduque

---
